mnemonics: For a cozy space, every sequence has a 'couch potato' subsequence that never leaves its comfy spot.
keyPoints: In a metric space, a subset is compact if and only if it is sequentially compact.
Compactness (every open cover has a finite subcover) is equivalent to sequential compactness (every sequence has a convergent subsequence).
This equivalence is a fundamental property of metric spaces and does not hold in general topological spaces.
commonMistakes: Assuming this equivalence holds in all topological spaces (it is specific to metric spaces).
Confusing the limit point of a subsequence with the limit point of the original sequence.
